moduleInfo.title: Capstone: Complete Personal Finance Tracker
moduleInfo.description: Build a complete Personal Finance Tracker application from scratch using FastAPI, PostgreSQL, and modern Python patterns.

findings.description: pyproject.toml example specifies requires-python as >=3.13 but the course target is Python 3.12. This should be >=3.12 to match the course requirements.
findings.details: The content shows 'requires-python = ">=3.13"' and '[tool.ruff] target-version = "py313"' along with '[tool.mypy] python_version = "3.13"'. All version references should target Python 3.12 to align with the course requirements.
findings.suggestion: Update all Python version references from 3.13 to 3.12 in the pyproject.toml example to match the course target version.

findings.description: Challenge ID uses inconsistent numbering format (module-22-lesson-01-challenge-1) when this is module 24.
findings.details: The challenge ID is 'module-22-lesson-01-challenge-1' but this is Module 24. This inconsistency could cause confusion when tracking challenges across modules.
findings.suggestion: Update challenge ID to 'module-24-lesson-01-challenge-1' for consistency with module numbering.

findings.description: conftest.py example uses pytest-asyncio patterns that may have compatibility issues with current versions.
findings.details: The fixture uses 'event_loop' pattern with pytest-asyncio which has changed in recent versions. Modern pytest-asyncio (0.24+) uses different fixture patterns and the 'asyncio_mode = auto' configuration in pyproject.toml may conflict with explicit event_loop fixtures.
findings.suggestion: Review and update pytest-asyncio fixture patterns to match current best practices. Consider removing explicit event_loop fixture when using asyncio_mode = auto.

findings.details: The 01-theory.md file contains no educational content - it appears to have placeholder content or minimal structure. The capstone deployment lesson should contain theory about deployment concepts.
findings.suggestion: Add comprehensive theory content covering deployment concepts for the capstone project, including Docker basics, environment configuration, and production considerations.
